metadata.disclaimer: Simplified teaching shapes, not archaeological boundaries. Edit this file or replace with your own GeoJSON from QGIS.
metadata.mapEras: Each feature.properties.mapEras lists timeline era ids (see ui.js ERAS). The era slider must match one id for the polygon to appear.

properties.name: Canaan / promised land (very rough)
properties.description: A single broad outline for narratives before the detailed tribal allotments (Genesis–Joshua).
properties.scripture: Genesis 12:7 — The Lord appeared to Abram and said, “To your offspring I will give this land.”
properties.notes: Use the United Kingdom era for Joshua-style tribal blocks; Divided Kingdom for Israel vs Judah.

properties.name: Tribe of Judah (Joshua allotment — simplified)
properties.description: Southern highlands and Negev footprint, stylized—not a cadastral survey.

scripture.text: The allotment for the tribe of Judah, according to its clans, extended toward the Desert of Zin to the border of Edom.

properties.name: Tribe of Benjamin
properties.description: Small central strip between Judah and Joseph blocks in this teaching model.

scripture.text: The first lot came up for the tribe of Benjamin according to its clans.

properties.name: Ephraim & western Manasseh (hill country)
properties.description: Combined Samarian hill country for clarity at this zoom level.

scripture.text: The allotment for Joseph began at the Jordan east of Jericho…

properties.name: Northern tribes (Galilee block — simplified)
properties.description: Stylized area for Asher, Naphtali, Zebulun, Issachar at small scale.

scripture.text: The fifth lot came out for the tribe of Asher according to its clans…

properties.name: Dan (coastal plain — simplified)
properties.description: Coastal foothills west of the hill country in this model.

scripture.text: The seventh lot came out for the tribe of Dan according to its clans.

properties.name: Reuben, Gad & half-Manasseh (Transjordan — simplified)
properties.description: East of the Jordan rift; highly generalized.

scripture.text: The other half of Manasseh, Reuben and Gad had received the inheritance that Moses had given them east of the Jordan.

properties.name: Northern Kingdom of Israel
properties.description: Approximate extent after the split (930 BC onward), not identical to later Assyrian provinces.

scripture.text: When all the Israelites heard that Jeroboam had returned, they sent for him and made him king over all Israel.
The king of Assyria invaded the entire land, marched against Samaria and laid siege to it for three years.

properties.name: Kingdom of Judah
properties.description: Southern kingdom with Jerusalem as capital in this simplified footprint.

scripture.text: Say to Rehoboam son of Solomon king of Judah…
So in the ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign… Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon marched against Jerusalem with his whole army.

properties.name: Province of Yehud (Persian era — schematic)
properties.description: Tiny returning community around Jerusalem and the Judean hills—exaggerated for visibility.

scripture.text: In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, in order to fulfill the word of the Lord spoken by Jeremiah, the Lord moved the heart of Cyrus king of Persia to make a proclamation.

properties.name: Galilee (Herodian / Roman — schematic)
properties.description: Administrative north for Gospel geography at small scale.

scripture.text: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, the Way of the Sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles…

properties.name: Judea (Roman — schematic)
properties.description: Southern district including Jerusalem in a stylized envelope.

scripture.text: In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah…

properties.name: Samaria (between Galilee and Judea — schematic)
properties.description: Central highlands corridor in New Testament administrative terms.

scripture.text: Now he had to go through Samaria.
